_meta.purpose: Plain-language descriptions used in R1 skill offers. Bilingual envelope: ar null falls back to en (output_gate translates the composed reply for Arabic sessions). When clinician-authored Khaleeji blurbs land, they become the verbatim display names echoed in offers and accept-classification.
review_required: Clinical review of every display_name and description before production
_meta._signed_off: EN blurbs enriched 2026-06-17 for informed choice (one-clause -> what it is, what you do, what it helps with, plus duration); approach clinician-approved (relayed via product owner), intensity-gating deferred. v0.2.0 EN wording clinician-approved 2026-06-13. ar pending S2-2 + two-rater Khaleeji scoring. Two-rater EN offer-turn QUALITY scoring remains a SEPARATE open gate.
_meta._normative_realignment_2026_07_10: OF-1 (BOT BEHAVIOUR.docx now normative). 5 canonical-source blurbs synced to the doc's One-line psychoeducation column (content verbatim, case-folded for mid-sentence offer grammar; house what-you-do + duration retained): box_breathing, grounding_5_4_3_2_1, stop_technique, progressive_muscle_relaxation, mindfulness_meditation. display_name realigned to normative doc names (skill_ids untouched; supersedes the 2026-06-13 name approval for these two): cbt_thought_record -> 'Fact vs. Opinion', values_clarification -> 'Life Compass'. The other 15 blurbs remain deliberate house style = PARTIAL, queued to a clinician sign-off packet, NOT re-approved here. HELD pending confirm (may not suit a user-facing offer label): interpersonal_effectiveness doc name 'DEARMAN' (technique acronym), financial_anxiety doc name 'Financial Anxiety (Gulf)' (category label).

description.en: a simple 4-count breathing pattern that slows your heart rate and signals safety to your nervous system, where you breathe in, hold, out, and hold again to an even count, about five minutes
uses your five senses to anchor your attention in the present moment instead of the anxious thought loop, naming what you can see, hear, and touch, takes a few minutes

a short pause-and-reframe technique to create space between a trigger and your reaction, where you stop, take a breath, notice what is happening and what you feel, then choose how to respond, about a minute
a fast physical reset for very intense moments, using cold water, brisk movement, and slower breathing to bring your body down from overwhelm quickly, about ten minutes
systematically tensing and releasing muscle groups to physically discharge built-up tension, gently tensing and then releasing each muscle group in turn, ten to fifteen minutes
a calming exercise where you picture a place, real or imagined, that feels safe and build it up in detail with your senses, so you can settle and return to it when you need calm, about ten minutes
a slow guided check-in where you move your attention gently through your body from head to toe, noticing sensation without judging it and releasing tension as you go, ten to fifteen minutes
a step by step way to take one heavy thought, look at the evidence for and against it, and reach a more balanced view, so a painful belief loosens its grip, ten to fifteen minutes
working through a stuck or self critical thought pattern, spotting the thinking trap inside it, and building a fairer, more workable way to see the situation, ten to fifteen minutes
picking one small, doable activity and planning together when and how you will do it, to gently rebuild momentum and lift your mood when everything feels flat, about ten minutes
a way to contain worries that follow you all day by giving them one short, fixed slot to be dealt with, so they stop taking over every hour, about ten minutes to set up
breaking one real problem into clear options, weighing them together, and choosing a first concrete step you can actually take, so a stuck situation starts to move, ten to fifteen minutes
going through what is getting in the way of your sleep and building a realistic wind down routine that fits your life, to help you fall asleep and stay asleep, about ten minutes
a short, gentle guided check-in on how you are really doing across your mood, energy, and what is on your mind, to help you name where you are right now, about five minutes
exploring what matters most to you underneath the day to day, so decisions and priorities get easier and line up better with the person you want to be, ten to fifteen minutes
preparing how to say a hard thing clearly and kindly, with a chance to practice the words, so you can speak up without it turning into a fight or backing down, ten to fifteen minutes
a structured way to prepare a difficult ask or set a boundary with someone, balancing the relationship and your own needs, so you go into it feeling ready, ten to fifteen minutes
a short guided practice for meeting a hard moment with the same kindness you would offer a friend, easing harsh self criticism when you are struggling, about five minutes
practicing how to notice difficult thoughts and feelings and let them come and go without being pushed around by them, so you can act on what matters even when it is hard, about ten minutes
a plain language walk through of how anxiety works in the mind and body, why it shows up, and what actually helps calm it, so it feels less frightening and more manageable, about ten minutes
a plain language walk through of how low mood can feed on itself, what keeps the loop going, and the small things that help break it, so it feels less baffling, about ten minutes
a plain language walk through of what stress does to your mind and body, why it builds up, and practical ways to bring it down, so you can spot and ease it sooner, about ten minutes
working through money stress step by step, separating the worry from the facts, and finding one thing within your control to act on, so it feels less paralysing, ten to fifteen minutes
a gentle, guided space to be with a loss at your own pace, with no pressure to fix it or move on, just room to feel it and put it into words, about fifteen minutes
a longer-form practice for sitting with anxious feelings without needing to fix or fight them, resting your attention on your breath and letting thoughts and feelings come and go, a few minutes
